info.title: hoodify-finance — GDS API
info.description:
The Game Development System (GDS) API — the build-methodology backend
(builders, tasks, claims, credits, grading, memory, dev boxes).

**API version:** `v1` — served at `/api/bongos/v1`. The
unversioned `/api/bongos` and legacy `/api/gds` are permanent aliases
pinned to this version; pin to `/api/bongos/v1` for a stable
contract (breaking changes ship a new `/vN`, ADR 0117). This is the
CONTRACT version, distinct from the package build in `info.version`.

**Base path:** `/api/bongos` (every path below is relative to it).

**Auth:** a builder session — the `gds_session` cookie (web) or
`Authorization: Bearer <token>` (CLI). **Ranks** gate writes and are
enforced server-side per request, never cached (ADR 0016):
`public` < `any-builder` < `metic+archon` < `archon`.
Each operation carries its required rank in `x-rank`.

Generated from the live route files by `scripts/gds/gen-api-docs.js` —
do not hand-edit; it regenerates on every ship.

servers.description: Versioned API base (same-origin) — pin here for a stable contract.
Unversioned alias (permanent) — resolves to the current version.

get.description:
GET /access-requests?status=pending — Archon queue. Defaults to pending; pass ?status=invited|dismissed for the resolved history. rank: archon — the queue exposes would-be builders' usernames.

**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

post.description:
POST /access-requests — PUBLIC. A would-be builder asks to be admitted. rank: public — the requester is by definition outside the system (no builder row yet); this is the one public write on the GDS.

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

get.description:
GET /access-requests/status?github_login=X — PUBLIC. The `bongos login` CLI polls this after filing a request: the device flow's not_approved is TERMINAL (the device_code is spent), so the CLI can't re-poll the flow — it polls this instead, then restarts sign-in once admitted. Exposes nothing the public POST above doesn't already reveal through its 409s (already_member / already_approved). status ∈ member | invited | pending | dismissed | none; admitted = member||invited. rank: public — a would-be builder is by definition outside the system. Declared BEFORE `GET /access-requests` so the literal `/status` path is matched as its own route (not swallowed as a query on the list route).

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

patch.description:
PATCH /access-requests/:id — Archon resolves a request. body: { status: 'invited' | 'dismissed' }. 'invited' = admitted to the hall (the only sign-in gate — no repo access needed); 'dismissed' = declined / spam / dup. The row stays as the audit trail either way. rank: archon — same gate as the rest of the roster management surface.

**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

get.description:
All seeded achievements — lets /builders render locked + unlocked side by side. rank: any-builder — global achievement catalog (locked + unlocked rendering).

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

a builder's historical AI dev curve. A builder reads their own id; an Archon reads anyone's (powers the cross-builder view).

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

GET /api/gds/audit-log?limit=&offset=&builder_id=&method= Paginated, newest-first. Clamps: limit → 1..500, default 100 offset → >= 0, default 0 rank: archon — forensics surface; only the Archon should see who did what.

**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

post.description:
(own identity only) — approving signs the app in AS THE APPROVER. Cookie-gated exactly like /auth/cli-token/issue above: a leaked CLI bearer must not be able to approve pairings and mint itself siblings; only the httpOnly browser cookie — the click in the hall — can.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

get.description:
read-only: what the approve page shows BEFORE the click (device name/platform for the given code). Unknown and expired are indistinguishable (404) so the code space can't be probed for liveness.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

post.description:
poll with the secret poll_token; the token IS the bearer (mirrors /auth/device/poll, where the device_code is the bearer). Kept LAST in this router so route-rank-check's look-ahead window stays clear of any neighboring gated declaration (see the block comment above).

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

starts a pairing; returns opaque codes only, no identity.

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

POST /auth/backchannel-logout — the INSTANCE side of federated single-logout (ADR 0144, task 1002238). The hub POSTs a hub-signed logout token here when a builder signs out of the hub; we verify it and revoke ALL of that builder's local sessions, so one hub logout ends the session on every federated project. rank: public — the hub-signed EdDSA logout token IS the credential (like the OAuth callback), NOT a builder session. Inert (404) on an instance that does not federate through a hub (idp unset), so a self-hosted instance exposes no new surface. Idempotent: an unknown/replayed subject just revokes nothing.

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

POST /auth/cli-token/issue — V3.R91 / task 401. Issue a fresh CLI bearer for the requesting builder, gated on a WEB COOKIE session (not a bearer). The browser already proves identity via the gds_session cookie; this endpoint mints a separate cli-source session row the builder can paste into ~/.config/otb/gds-session.json via scripts/gds/paste-token.js. Replaces the ~30-60s Device Flow when the CLI bearer has expired. rank: any-builder (own identity only) — no rank gate. auth source restriction: cookie ONLY. A bearer cannot mint a new bearer (a leaked CLI token shouldn't be able to extend its own lifetime; the httpOnly browser cookie is the harder-to-steal credential).

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

POST /auth/device/exchange — CLI federation (ADR 0141 §1). The CLI ran the device flow against the HUB and got a hub-signed assertion; it presents it here. The instance verifies the Ed25519 signature OFFLINE against the provisioned hub public key (aud must equal this instance's client_id, unexpired), then runs the instance's OWN admission gate + upsertBuilder + createSession(source='cli') and returns the existing { token, builder }. Only reachable when auth.idp is set. rank: public — an auth-establishment endpoint; the hub-signed assertion IS the bearer and is cryptographically verified before any session is minted.

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

auth flow; the device code itself is the bearer.

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

/auth/device — CLI flow rank: public — auth flow itself; identity establishment, not authorization.

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

get.description:
completes the link to the caller's own identity.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

/auth/discord — account-linking OAuth (Phase 3 F2, ADR 0033 §2). Links a Discord account to the ALREADY-AUTHENTICATED builder (gds_session cookie): the link is self-asserted by the builder, never invented by the bot. Inert (503) until the server has Discord client creds. Mirrors the GitHub web flow's CSRF-state discipline above — a hard requirement (ADR 0033 §2). rank: any-builder — links to your OWN identity; no rank gate.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

post.description:
unlink your OWN Discord. JSON (called from the hall UI). F7 follow-up: also kicks the builder from the guild so they can't remain in the server without a linked account. The kick is best-effort — the unlink always succeeds even if the bot lacks Kick Members or the member already left.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

own session, no rank gate (a Xenos can log themselves out).

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

POST /auth/revoke — V3.R38 / task 259. Archon-only. Kill a SINGLE session by its primary-key id. Used for surgical lockouts (e.g. a compromised CLI on a known builder's laptop) without disturbing that builder's other sessions. Body: { session_id: <bigint> }. Response: { ok, revoked: 0|1 }. rank: archon — kills an arbitrary session; can lock other builders out.

**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

POST /auth/revoke-all — V3.R38 / task 259. Archon-only. Kill EVERY active session for a given builder. Used at demotion (auto-called from setBuilderRank when newRank moves down the ladder — C7 rank-change-near- instant) and as a manual operator hammer. Body: { builder_id: <bigint> }. Response: { ok, revoked: <count> }. rank: archon — same authority as PATCH /builders/:id/rank.

**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

OAuth callback; the state cookie + code are the bearer.

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

/auth/web — browser flow (used by the /builders page). rank: public — entry into the GitHub OAuth handshake.

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.
**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

any working builder may check or trigger a backup.

**Rank:** `metic+archon` — Metic or Archon rank (review/triage powers).

get.description:
List currently-open blockers + the tasks each is blocking. Public: anyone landing on /builders or status.amazonprimea.com can see what's stuck on Lars. rank: public — open blocker list; documented "Public: anyone landing on /builders or status.amazonprimea.com can see what's stuck on Lars" in the handler comment above.

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

post.description:
FILE a blocker (contribution, not resolution).

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

get.description:
single blocker detail read.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

post.description:
Link an existing blocker to a task (puts the task in 'blocked' status if it's currently 'backlog' or 'ready'). Metic+ (task 360 / ADR 0018) — same blocker-review grant as resolve above. rank: metic+archon — link a blocker to a task; same blocker-review grant as resolve.

**Rank:** `metic+archon` — Metic or Archon rank (review/triage powers).

Resolve. Auto-promotes any blocked tasks linked via task_blockers. Metic+ (task 360 / ADR 0018): blocker review is one of the operations C2 assigns to Metic, so this widened from archon-only to metic+archon when the three-rank model went live. Any builder can still FILE a blocker (POST /blockers above); a Xenos cannot resolve or link them. rank: metic+archon — blocker review (auto-promotes linked blocked tasks); criterion C2 / task 360 / ADR 0018.

**Rank:** `metic+archon` — Metic or Archon rank (review/triage powers).

get.description:
GET /box/authorized-keys — the box pulls its builder's authorized_keys. rank: any authenticated builder (own resource) — but the ALLOW/DENY is decided INSIDE from the caller's LIVE rank+status (boxAccess.decideSourceAccess), NOT a static requireRank, mirroring /box/source-access: a demoted/deactivated builder is 403'd and the box-side fetch empties the managed authorized_keys block on the very next cron — instant SSH-login revocation (ADR 0016 extended to login). The body is text/plain: the managed key LINES (no markers — the box wraps them). An allowed builder with no registered keys gets an empty 200 (a valid empty block). task 919: allowBoxScope — box-authorized-keys-fetch.sh (cron */10) runs box-scoped.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

post.description:
POST /box/close — the builder manually requests their box be deprovisioned. rank: any authenticated builder (own resource). Enqueues a 'deprovision' intent; the control-plane runner executes it so the web tier never holds DO_API_TOKEN (trust boundary holds, same as /box/ensure). Only valid for an active box — a box that isn't running has nothing to close.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

get.description:
GET /box/connect — the human/Claude-readable connect walkthrough. rank: public — no auth on purpose: a brand-new builder fetches this from a bare laptop (no token yet) to learn the one command that onboards them. It contains no secrets, only instructions + the public bootstrap URLs.

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

GET /box/connect.ps1 — the Windows (PowerShell) bootstrap script. rank: public — same posture as connect.sh (non-secret installer; self-auths).

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

GET /box/connect.sh — the macOS/Linux bootstrap script. rank: public — non-secret installer (the rustup/nvm pattern). It does its OWN GitHub device-flow auth before it touches anything; serving it needs no auth.

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

post.description:
POST /box/ensure — "make my box ready". rank: any authenticated builder (own resource) — the ALLOW/DENY + the chosen ACTION are decided INSIDE from the caller's LIVE rank+status (boxes.decideEnsureAction), NOT a static requireRank, mirroring /box/source-access. This single call covers BOTH halves of task 701's gap: a builder with no box gets a provision queued (auto-provision), a builder whose box is parked gets a wake queued (wake-on-connect). It enqueues at most one open intent per builder; the actual DO work happens on the control plane.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

POST /box/heartbeat — the box reports it is alive, resetting the idle clock. rank: any authenticated builder (own resource write). Optional body: { claude_active: bool } — when provided, records whether a `claude` process is running. The idle sweep skips boxes with claude_active=true, so an active Claude session keeps the box alive indefinitely. Heartbeats without a body behave as before (last_activity_at bumped, claude_active unchanged). task 919: allowBoxScope — box-heartbeat.sh (cron */5) runs with the box-scoped session.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

POST /box/host-keys — the box publishes its own PUBLIC SSH host keys (task 1187 / idea 235). rank: any authenticated builder (own resource) — the BOX calls this as its builder via the GDS token on the box; allowBoxScope so the box-scoped baked session (ADR 0053) can reach it, exactly like box-report-terminal.sh / the heartbeat reporter. Stored ONLY for an active box (setBoxHostKeys gates on it). Served back to the owner via GET /box/me; the connect path writes them to known_hosts authoritatively (no TOFU, no keyscan race), killing the post-re-provision "Host denied". host_keys are PUBLIC — never a secret — but the write is validated hard (anti-injection, >=1 ed25519).

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

get.description:
GET /box/managed-settings — the caller's Claude Desktop managed-settings.json. rank: any authenticated builder (own resource; non-secret — just their box hostname + the SSH identity path). Issued regardless of box state: the builder needs it on disk BEFORE the operator provisions, then connects once the box is up.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

GET /box/me — the caller's own box. rank: any authenticated builder (own resource read). Returns { box: { state:'none' } } when they have no box yet.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

GET /box/source-access — the box fetches its rank-scoped clone spec + the credential to pull it (ADR 0031 §6, task 600). rank: any authenticated builder (own resource) — the ALLOW/DENY and the SCOPE are decided INSIDE from the caller's LIVE rank+status, NOT by a static requireRank: a Xenos must still get their 'starter' credential, a Metic+ gets 'full', and a below-floor / inactive builder is denied (403). This is ADR 0016 server-enforcement extended from "writes are gated" to "source is gated": the box presents the BUILDER's session, the GDS reads the live DB rank (unforgeable from the box), so a demoted/deactivated builder is cut off on their VERY NEXT fetch — the instant, credential-layer half of the offboarding clawback (the droplet teardown is the slower control-plane half via `box.js reconcile`). The credential is returned ONLY here, from the prod env (never the repo — ADR 0022), and is NEVER logged or exposed by publicBox()/the roster. task 919: allowBoxScope — one of the 4 endpoints the on-box crons call, so a box-scoped session (source='box') is permitted here. box-source-fetch.sh (cron */10).

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

GET /box/ssh-key — list the caller's registered keys. rank: any builder (own).

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

post.description:
POST /box/ssh-key — register the caller's own public SSH key. rank: any authenticated builder (own resource write). The key is validated + normalized (box-onboard.validateSshPublicKey) so nothing attacker-shaped can reach the box's authorized_keys; the audit_log middleware records the write.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

delete.description:
DELETE /box/ssh-key/:id — remove one of the caller's keys. rank: any builder (own resource; the delete is builder_id-scoped in SQL so an id from another builder cannot be removed). 404 when the id isn't the caller's.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

get.description:
GET /box/terminal — the caller reads their box's web-terminal URL + credential (the Chromebook on-ramp, ADR 0038: ttyd behind a Cloudflare Tunnel). rank: any authenticated builder (own resource). Read-only by design: it does NOT wake a parked box (that mutation belongs to POST /box/ensure). The Chromebook flow is: POST /box/ensure → poll this until state='active' and a url appears → open the url, enter the basic-auth credential, run `claude` in the terminal. The url AND credential are returned ONLY to the box's own builder, and are null until the box's terminal reporter (box-report-terminal.sh) has published them.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

post.description:
POST /box/terminal — the box publishes its current web-terminal URL + the per-box basic-auth credential. rank: any authenticated builder (own resource) — the BOX calls this as its builder, using the GDS token already on the box. Both are stored ONLY when the box is active (boxes.setTerminalAccess enforces this), so a stale URL/credential can never be served after a park. The URL is validated to a bounded https URL; the credential is a bounded opaque string (a secret), only ever returned to its own builder (GET /box/terminal). task 919: allowBoxScope — box-report-terminal.sh (credsync) runs box-scoped. The GET above is the builder reading from the hall, so it stays full-auth (not box-scoped).

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

POST /box/version — the box reports its /workspace HEAD commit so the server can flag a stale box (idea 332 / task 1316, ADR 0072). Body: { sha: "<40-hex>", committed_at: "<ISO date>" } rank: any-builder — own resource (the box reports its OWN code). task 919 pattern: allowBoxScope so the on-box cron (box-report-version.sh, box-scoped session) can call it, exactly like /box/host-keys + /box/heartbeat. Non-secret.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

get.description:
GET /boxes — full roster across all builders. rank: archon (exposes every builder's box + IP + cost; same gate as the rest of roster management).

**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

GET /boxes/cost-ledger — the full per-builder container-cost ledger + totals (task 602). rank: archon — cost oversight across every builder without a DB query. Declared among the archon routes (archon-first) so the route-rank auditor's 20-line lookahead classifies it correctly.

**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

patch.description:
PATCH /boxes/:builderId/block — Archon bars (or un-bars) a builder from OPENING dev boxes (idea 257 / task 1163; migration 104). Body: { blocked: bool (required), reason?: string, // recorded on block; shown back to the builder close_current?: bool } // on block, ALSO deprovision a running box Block alone is "no new opens" (decideEnsureAction → BOX_PROVISION_BLOCKED); it does NOT touch a box that is already active. close_current adds the explicit teardown the owner asked be optional ("options for both"). rank: archon. Archon-first ordering (see above).

**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

post.description:
POST /boxes/:builderId/close — Archon force-closes ANY builder's box (idea 257 / task 1163). Trust-safe by the same mechanism as the builder's own POST /box/close: it ENQUEUES a 'deprovision' intent and the control-plane runner executes it, so the web tier never holds DO_API_TOKEN (ADR 0031 §9.4). rank: archon (acts on another builder's paid infrastructure). Declared among the archon routes (archon-first) so the route-rank auditor's 20-line lookahead classifies it correctly.

**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

get.description:
GET /api/bongos/bug-attachments/:name — stream one stored image.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

GET /builders/directory — the any-builder-readable picker roster (task 1877): every ACTIVE builder's identity, for a client-side invite dropdown (the Goals invite control, task 1762) that shouldn't be limited to GET /leaderboard's top-100-by-credits window. Deliberately narrower than /builders/roster — no onboarding/stale-claim/box data, which stays Archon-only; id/login/name/ avatar/rank is no more sensitive than what /leaderboard already exposes publicly, just without the credits cutoff. rank: any-builder.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

GET /builders/me/pending-rank-change — V3.R28 / task 249 ceremony signal task 3. Returns the calling builder's most recent rank-change credit_log row, or { change: null } when they've never had a rank change. The local SessionStart hook (`.claude/hooks/rank-change-greeting.js`) calls this on every session start and dedupes via a local "seen" marker — so the server doesn't need to track acknowledgment. That means this endpoint is a pure read; it never mutates anything. Why this lives on /builders/me/* (not /me/*): the route is part of the ceremony surface that V3.R28's touches[] explicitly covers (src/bongos/routes/builders.js). Routing it under /builders avoids editing /me.js (outside touches[]) while keeping the URL semantically clean. rank: any-builder — own rank-change history; mirrors /me/* posture.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

GET /builders/roster — V3.R14 (task 238) onboarding observability. Archon-only roster of every builder with their identity + onboarding signals + active-claim posture. Powers the management panel in the /builders hall so the Archon can promote / demote / release-stale-claim without dropping into psql. Optional query param: ?stale_hours=N (default 24). Defines what counts as "stale" for the per-row stale_claim flag — mirrors the threshold the stale-claim-release sweeper uses but exposed as a roster knob so the Archon can dial it in if needed. rank: archon — surfaces every builder's onboarding state.

**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

GET /builders/:id/active-claims — V3.R14 (task 238). Archon-only detail view of a single builder's active claims, with the same stale_claim flag the roster computes. The roster only carries boolean "has any stale claim?"; this endpoint surfaces *which* claim is stale so the Archon can target the release. rank: archon — privileged read of another builder's work surface.

**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

patch.description:
PATCH /builders/:id/budget — set or clear a builder's monthly spend ceiling (V3.R84 task 302, criterion C3). Archon-only. Body: { monthly_budget_usd: <num> } sets the per-builder override — the manual emergency brake; a positive value engages the warn/over thresholds. { monthly_budget_usd: null } clears it: the builder becomes UNCAPPED (there is no default cap — ADR 0085). This is the "budget per-builder configurable by Archon" done-when. 404 if no such builder. rank: archon — changes another builder's cost ceiling.

**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

get.description:
GET /builders/:id/onboarding — V3.R75 (task 294) onboarding checklist read. Returns the builder's onboarding state machine row, shaped for the /builders hall checklist UI: { builder_id, current_stage, completed_stages, started_at, completed_at, progress: { cleared, total }, stages: [{ id, label, hint, cleared, is_current }, ...], graduated: boolean } Public read (no auth). Spec says the hall renders other builders' checklists read-only — a builder's onboarding progress is roster-visible signal, same posture as rank or drachmae shown on the leaderboard. The builder's own row is also surfaced inside /api/gds/me; this endpoint is what the hall hits for the CURRENT viewer AND for any other builder whose card is rendered. rank: public — onboarding progress is a roster-visible signal.

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

GET /builders/:id/profile — the viewable builder profile page's read (task 1467, goal 25 "bx-identity"). Returns ONE builder's public portfolio: identity + rank + economy counters + shipped-work count + recent ships + unlocked achievements. Backs the shareable /builders/profile page. rank: public — every field returned here is ALREADY world-readable: the leaderboard (GET /public/leaderboard, no auth) exposes rank, total_credits, karma, and the top achievement ids; GET /builders/:id/onboarding is likewise an unauthenticated roster-visible read. This route is a per-builder re- presentation of the SAME already-public data, so a portfolio link is shareable without a sign-in wall (that IS the feature). It deliberately WHITELISTS the public column subset off the full builder row — the private columns getBuilderById also returns (anthropic_email / anthropic_user_id / monthly_budget_usd / system_role / box_blocked*) are NEVER spread into the JSON. A leak-prevention unit test (tests/profile_route.mjs) asserts their absence + the 404-on-unknown-id behaviour.

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

patch.description:
sole chokepoint that mutates builders.rank; ADR 0016 §enforcement.

**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

PATCH /builders/:id/status — graceful exit + reactivation (V3.R88 task 307). Archon-only. { status: 'inactive', reason? } deactivates (releases active claims, seals their session_logs, rank → xenos, blocks new claims while preserving all credit/history); { status: 'active' } reactivates. Cannot change your own status (no last-Archon footgun — same guard as /rank). rank: archon — deactivation/reactivation; preserves credit history but blocks claims.

**Rank:** `archon` — Archon only (the single owner — ratification, infra, rank changes).

post.description:
own work (claim a ready task); Xenos additionally filtered to newcomer_friendly rows inside db.claimTask (returns rank_too_low_for_task 403). Supervisor hot path (preserved per CRITICAL §4 — no requireRank). gateNewcomerClaim enforces the newcomer focus rule — a Xenos must ship their current claim before opening another (task 766; the prior primer gate was removed there). task 1642: gateOneActiveClaimPerSession is the session-scoped, all-rank, FAIL-CLOSED hard block on a second active claim in the same session (one worktree → one claim → no commingled ships). It runs BEFORE the Xenos-only gateNewcomerClaim, which stays for its rank-graduation messaging.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

Atomic multi-claim. Body: { task_ids: [n,n,n], worktree_name?: string }. Refused while the task holds an active claim — mid-claim touches rewriting is the drift-expand task 879 removed; flag_release instead. All targets must belong to THIS goal (containment — the parent-attach precedent: a manager can't reshape another goal's tasks through this route). rank: any-builder (requireBuilder) + the in-handler (owner | manager | Archon) gate (authorizeGoalManagement). post.description:
POST /goals/:id/tasks — bounded task authoring within a goal's scope (BV1.R60 / ADR 0086 §3). THE ENFORCEMENT KEYSTONE of the goal-scoped work hierarchy: the one route that lets a member (even a sub-Metic one) author their OWN work — bounded by the goal's module wall, and never able to mint work they couldn't otherwise be trusted to claim. requireBuilder + the in-handler, ORDERED, FAIL-CLOSED gate (authorizeGoalTaskCreate above). On pass, db.createTask stamps goal_id with requires_rank AUTO-DERIVED (ADR 0084) — NEVER taken from client input: a protected/sensitive task auto-floors to Metic, so a sub-Metic author simply cannot claim it (there is no self-granted escape). The task's version is the GOAL's version (never client-supplied) and it is born 'ready' so the author can immediately claim + work it — the maneuverability grant (the scope wall + auto-rank-floor already bound WHAT they may create). One-level sub-tasks attach via hierarchy.attachParent (the depth-cap + version-match authority), constrained to the SAME goal. rank: any-builder (requireBuilder) + goal membership + the scope wall. Pinned in route-rank-check.EXPECTED_RANKS['lifecycle'] by BV1.R61.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

The DB move is atomic: created_by reassigns and the new owner's membership upserts to 'lead' in one statement; the OLD owner keeps their lead row — they drop to manager, not out. rank: any-builder (requireBuilder) + the in-handler (owner | Archon) gate. post.description:
----------------------------------------------------------------------- BFG CROSS-BUILDER WRITE (task 446 / ADR 0026 §6C) — the FIRST cross-builder memory write path, the deliberate bounded exception to ADR 0016. Reachable ONLY by the BFG service principal (requireBfgPrincipal — an IDENTITY gate on system_role='bfg', NOT a rank gate, so an Archon/stolen token is rejected), ONLY when the kill-switch BFG_WRITE_ENABLED is on, and EVERY write lands a fail-closed audit row in the SAME transaction as the write (memory.bfgWrite- MemoryFile). Writes are namespaced (bfg/<kind>-<slug>.md) + self-announcing (frontmatter author: BFG) so a BFG note can never masquerade as the builder's own. The builder can always see and delete it. Rate limiting: this is a small write (one file), so the GLOBAL per-session/ per-IP write limiter (middleware/rate-limit.js, applied to every GDS write) already throttles a runaway loop. Unlike /memory/sync — which carries up to 50 MB and needed a tighter dedicated cap — this endpoint doesn't warrant its own limiter, especially as it's principal-only + kill-switched. POST /memory/builders/:builderId/bfg-write body: { kind: 'dream'|'correction', slug, title?, body_md, rationale } 201 → { ok, builder_id, path, kind, author:'BFG', audit_id, sha256, byte_len, updated_at } Declaration opener kept on ONE line (route path beside router.post) so the deterministic route-rank auditor (src/bongos/route-rank-check.js) detects + classifies it; it reads requireBuilder in the chain below → 'any-builder', and requireBfgPrincipal narrows that to the principal alone.

**Rank:** `bfg-principal` — The BFG cross-builder memory service principal (not a human rank).

get.description:
GET /memory/file?path=... — fetch ONE of the AUTHENTICATED builder's files (content included). Single query (fetchMemoryFile is one SELECT). 404 if this builder has no file at that path. We use a ?path= query rather than a path-segment param because the stored keys are relative paths that may contain '/' (e.g. 'sub/dir/notes.md'); a single :path segment can't carry a slash, and a wildcard segment re-opens the traversal surface that assertValidPath exists to close. The query value is validated by assertValidPath (no leading '/', no '..', no NUL, no backslash) before it ever reaches the store. Auth: any authenticated builder. Self-scoped: owner is req.builder.id. Response 200: { builder_id, path, content, sha256, byte_len, mtime, updated_at, created_at }

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

delete.description:
DELETE /memory/file?path=... — the SELF delete affordance (ADR 0026 §6C). A builder removes ONE of their OWN files — INCLUDING a BFG-written dream/correction, which lives in this builder's keyspace. Owner is ALWAYS req.builder.id (assertSelf); there is no parameter to delete another builder's file, so this can't be a cross-builder delete. This is what makes a BFG write reversible by its recipient (the load-bearing consent property). Single-line opener so the route-rank auditor classifies it ('any-builder').

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

get.description:
GET /memory/files — list the AUTHENTICATED builder's memory files (metadata only, no content). Single query (listMemoryFiles is one SELECT — no N+1). Returns newest-changed first. Auth: any authenticated builder (requireBuilder). Self-scoped: owner is req.builder.id. NO owner param is accepted. Response 200: { builder_id, count, files: [ { path, sha256, byte_len, mtime, updated_at, created_at }, ... ] }

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

post.description:
POST /memory/sync — push a builder's local memory files to the server. Auth: any authenticated builder (requireBuilder). A builder can only ever write their OWN memory — the owner is req.builder.id, never from input. Request body (two accepted shapes): { files: [ { path, content, mtime? }, ... ] } (preferred) [ { path, content, mtime? }, ... ] (bare array, also ok) where: path — string, relative path under the memory dir (e.g. 'MEMORY.md'). Validated by storeMemoryFile (no leading '/', no '..', no NUL). content — string, the file body (UTF-8). mtime — optional ISO-8601 string or epoch ms; the source file's mtime. Stored for the pull side's newer-than check. Server clock is the conflict authority (last-write-wins), so this is advisory. Response 200 (always 200 when the request itself was well-formed; per-file outcomes are in the body so one bad file doesn't sink the batch): { stored: <count of files upserted>, skipped: <count of files skipped — e.g. non-string content>, errors: [ { path, code, message, hint? }, ... ], results: [ { path, stored: true, sha256, byte_len, updated_at } | { path, error: {...} } ] } A quota/oversize failure on a file is reported in errors[] with code FILE_TOO_LARGE / MEMORY_QUOTA_EXCEEDED and an HTTP 413 IF ANY file tripped a quota (so the client knows to prune), otherwise HTTP 200. Malformed paths (BAD_PATH) are per-file errors and do NOT force a non-200 — they're a client bug, not a quota signal.

**Rank:** `any-builder` — Any authenticated builder (row-level ownership enforced in-handler).

GET /provisioning/github-app/callback?code=&state= — GitHub redirects the owner's BROWSER here after they approve the App Manifest (task 2080, ADR 0133). It is a browser redirect with no session guaranteed, so it is PUBLIC — the unguessable, single-use, TTL'd `state` token (minted only for the signed-in owner and matched against provisioning_oauth_manifests) is the authorization, exactly as GitHub's own conversion `code` is "the code IS the auth". The web tier ONLY stores the code + flips status → 'code_received'; the control-plane runner (the sole token-holder) exchanges it for the client secret and places it in web.env. The secret NEVER touches the browser or the web tier. rank: public — no session, no mutation of authority, no secret revealed; a bad/expired/replayed state is a no-op error page.

Per-IP rate-limited because the page polls it every ~3s. `ready` is true iff the manifest reached 'done' (creds placed + /healthz verified) — the page then auto-redirects to https://<domain>. rank: public — no session, no mutation, no secret; an unknown state is a 404 no-op.

GET /provisioning/tls-check?domain=<host> — the Caddy on-demand-TLS ALLOW-LIST (ADR 0111 §4, task P4 task 1945). Caddy's global `on_demand_tls { ask … }` calls this before fetching a Let's Encrypt cert for an SNI it doesn't already have: HTTP 200 authorizes, any non-200 refuses. We authorize ONLY a hostname this module actually provisioned + is still serving (provisioning|active), so on-demand TLS can never be abused to mint certs for arbitrary names (the cert-flood guard). FAILS CLOSED — any error denies (503). rank: public (Caddy calls it locally over 127.0.0.1 with no session; a plain read, no mutation, no secret — it only reveals whether a host is a live provisioned instance). RATE-LIMITED (SEC review of task 1945). The route is reachable from the internet via the apex proxy, and core's global limiter bypasses reads while the 120/min public-read cap is mounted only under /public — this module mounts under /api/bongos, so it inherits neither. Without a limiter the endpoint is an unthrottled enumeration oracle + issuance-load amplifier. Core's limiter isn't reachable from a module (the one-way import rule; and the kernel doorway may not re-export it — it's a non-kernel core file, ADR 0091 §1), so we apply a dedicated, tight in-module limiter (askRateLimit). Caddy's own on_demand_tls interval/burst throttle is NOT an option (removed in Caddy 2.x — the `ask` endpoint IS the throttle and must self-limit).

**Rank:** `public` — No authentication — any caller.

post.description:
POST /search — two modes (ADR 0095 C1 / task 1658, progressive disclosure): 1. SUMMARY (default): body { q: string, limit?: 1..50, source_kind? }. Returns the ranked compact layer — each hit carries a snippet plus `snippet_tokens` (what you already see) and `full_tokens` (what a full expansion would COST), so the caller sees the price before paying it. 2. EXPAND: body { expand: <chunkId> } (with q optional). Returns that one chunk's FULL body + `body_tokens`, scoped exactly like search (rank + owner from the session), so expand can't widen visibility. Both shapes stay backward-compatible: the old { q } request still returns { query, count, results } — the new per-hit fields are additive. Single-line opener so the deterministic route-rank auditor classifies the chain (requireBuilder → 'any-builder') — ADR 0016 / route-rank-check.js.

POST /search/mark — the write half of the memory-usefulness feedback loop (ADR 0095 C3, task 1660). Body { chunk_id: int, useful?: bool }. Records that a recalled chunk did (useful=true, the default) or did NOT (useful=false) help, so the ranker lifts the useful and fades the dead-end. SCOPING (ADR 0016): the caller's id + rank come ONLY from the session, never the body — searchChunks-equivalent scope is re-applied in the UPDATE WHERE, so a chunk the caller couldn't have seen can't be voted on (→ updated:false). Single-line opener so the route-rank auditor classifies the chain.

The client is resolved from the USER-TYPED instance ORIGIN via the registry (getClientByOrigin) and BOUND to the device flow — the CLI never names the audience. This closes the confused-deputy where a malicious instance manifest could steer the mint at a victim client_id (ADR 0141 §1 security fix). Runs the GitHub device flow against the HUB's app and returns the codes. Identity is established as a platform ACCOUNT at poll time — never a hub-builder session (ADR 0141: identity federated, authority local). rank: public — an authentication entry point; the origin is public, and the poll's GitHub approval is the real gate.

The abandon + its `reason` land in audit_log via the writing middleware, so the ledger keeps a record of who retired what. rank: archon — terminal lifecycle mutation / scope removal (ADR 0016). Without this, an ENFORCING ADR-0058 gate-review BLOCKS a Metic+ author's gate-surface PR shipped from a laptop and they can't self-approve their own PR (hit live on task 1162 / PR task 131). AUTH mirrors publish-branch EXACTLY: requireBuilder + gateTaskOwnership, and deliberately NOT allowBoxScope (a box-scoped session 403s — it must reauth). The attestation power is bounded: gate-author-trust clears a NEEDS_TRUST surface for a Metic+ author only and NEVER the hard floor (which always needs an owner review), so a Metic+ vouching for their own task's head stays within their tier.

builderSession.name: gds_session
builderSession.description: Builder session. Web clients send the `gds_session` cookie; CLI clients send `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. Rank is resolved from the live builder record on every request (ADR 0016).

code.description: Stable machine-readable slug the caller may switch on (e.g. `task_not_found`). Classified into a category (`x-error-categories`); the specific slug is preserved (ADR 0116).

message.description: Human-readable, safe to surface. Never leaks internals unless a handler opts in.

details.description: Optional structured payload — e.g. the validator's `[{ field, reason }]` list.

ApiErrorObject.description: The inner error object carried by every non-2xx response.

ErrorEnvelope.description: The single error shape returned by EVERY non-2xx response (ADR 0116). A caller reads `error.code`.

limit.description: Page size actually applied (clamped to the endpoint cap, ADR 0119).

offset.description: Zero-based row offset of this page.

total.description: Total matching rows, when cheaply countable; derive "more pages" via `offset + items.length < total`.

PageInfo.description: The `page` block of a paginated list response (ADR 0119), built by `pageMeta()`.

StringifiableId.description: A resource id — an integer, or its stringified form (a Postgres bigint serializes as a JSON string, so ids may arrive either way). Accept both.

BadRequest.description: Malformed or invalid request (the ADR-0116 error envelope).

ValidationFailed.description: Request body failed schema validation; `error.details` lists the field errors.

Forbidden.description: Authenticated but rank/ownership too low for this route.

NotFound.description: The referenced resource does not exist.

bad_request.description: Malformed or invalid request (bad param, missing field, wrong type).

validation_failed.description: Request failed spec/schema validation; `details` lists the field errors.

forbidden.description: Authenticated, but rank/ownership does not permit the action.

not_found.description: The referenced resource does not exist.

conflict.description: Request conflicts with current state (already exists, cycle, active claim, stale tip).

payload_too_large.description: Request body/element exceeds the size limit.

rate_limited.description: Too many requests / quota exhausted; back off and retry.

internal.description: Unexpected server error or a server-side operation that failed.

bad_gateway.description: An upstream dependency returned an invalid response.

unavailable.description: A required dependency/feature is not configured or is temporarily down.
